title: Serve stale-while-revalidate for resilience
description: Use stale-while-revalidate and stale-if-error so users get fast cached responses while refresh happens in the background.
body: Use when freshness can tolerate brief staleness and availability matters. Do not force synchronous revalidation on every near-expiry request — it adds origin latency to the user path. Actions: set stale-while-revalidate so the edge serves stale content and refreshes asynchronously, and stale-if-error to survive origin outages. Validation signals include low user-facing latency at expiry boundaries and continued serving during an origin error. Risk boundary: do not use stale windows for data that must be exact (prices, balances) — escalate to no-store or short max-age there.
